message:
chore(beads): close ft-uic0x — windows alt-screen explicit error landed in 33dab3e2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -433,7 +433,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-twnmf","title":"[MEDIUM] br-43bbl WebGPU readback polling rewrite is untested at the changed timeout/callback boundary","description":"Commit 6f41130c rewrote WebGpuTexture::read() from a single wgpu::PollType::Wait call to a manual Poll loop with 10ms recv_timeout slices (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:163-193). That changes the control flow around map_async completion, timeout expiry, and callback disconnect handling, but the test module in the same file still only covers helper routines like padded_readback_bytes_per_row and copy_padded_readback_to_image (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:738-1003) and never exercises read() itself. This means a regression in the new bounded-poll behavior can land without any automated signal. Add a focused regression harness or extracted helper test that proves the success, timeout, and disconnected-callback cases for the new loop.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:54:02.292808Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:05.990393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:05.990145Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-tzwuw","title":"mcp_tools ca.search/ca.view/ca.status accept timeout_secs=0, fire immediate-timeout","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs\` cass tools (ca.search line
590, ca.view line 683, ca.status line 763) all pass
\`params.timeout_secs\` directly to \`cass_client_with_timeout()\` without
validating that it's >= 1.

The JSON-Schema declarations for these tools advertise
\`"timeout_secs": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 600,
"default": 15 }\` (lines 526, 636, 726), but \`serde_json::from_value\`
does not enforce JSON-Schema bounds — it happily deserializes \`0\` as
a valid u64.

## Concrete failure path

1. MCP client (or any caller that doesn't pre-validate against the
   schema) sends \`{"query": "x", "timeout_secs": 0}\` to ca.search.
2. \`serde_json::from_value\` accepts the 0 → \`params.timeout_secs = 0\`.
3. \`cass_client_with_timeout(0)\` constructs a CassClient with
   \`timeout = Duration::from_secs(0)\` (cass.rs:781-784).
4. \`run_with_cx\` (cass.rs:961) calls
   \`timeout_with_cx(cx, Duration::from_secs(0), cmd.output()).await\`
   — a zero-duration timeout fires immediately, before \`cmd.output()\`
   has any chance to execute.
5. Returns \`CassError::Timeout { timeout_secs: 0 }\` to the MCP client
   on EVERY call. The client sees a confusing "cass timeout (0 secs)"
   error and may not realize the issue is its own \`timeout_secs: 0\`
   parameter rather than a slow cass binary.

Same defect shape as ft-t62hq (already fixed for wa.wait_for and
wa.send tools at lines 1149, 1873) — three sibling cass tools were
missed in that sweep.

## Severity rationale

- Silent UX failure: caller's request always times out without ever
  invoking the underlying tool.
- Reachable via any client that doesn't validate against the
  advertised schema (in practice: most language SDKs that just pass
  through user-supplied JSON, plus any test fixture that omits
  timeout_secs default-checking).
- Not a crash, not data corruption.

## Fix sketch

Mirror the ft-t62hq guard at the top of each \`call(\)\` for the three
cass tools:

\`\`\`rust
if params.timeout_secs == 0 {
    let envelope = McpEnvelope::<()>::error(
        MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS,
        "timeout_secs must be >= 1 (got 0)".to_string(),
        Some(
            "The ca.<tool> schema declares timeout_secs with \\
             minimum: 1; omit the field to use the default (15)."
                .to_string(),
        ),
        elapsed_ms(start),
    );
    return envelope_to_content(envelope);
}
\`\`\`

## Regression tests

Three integration-style tests at the params layer:
- \`ca_search_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`
- \`ca_view_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`
- \`ca_status_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`

Each invokes the tool with \`timeout_secs: 0\` and asserts the response
is an Error envelope with \`code = MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS\` and the
"timeout_secs must be >= 1" message — proving the guard fires before
\`cass_client_with_timeout\` is ever called.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs\` only. No schema changes —
the schemas already declare \`minimum: 1\`. No changes to \`cass.rs\` —
\`with_timeout_secs(0)\` is a documented "instant timeout" primitive
that other callers may legitimately want.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:39:11.699057Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:15.687876Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:15.687498Z","close_reason":"Landed in db5bfeaf (fix(mcp_tools): reject timeout_secs=0 in ca.{search,view,status})","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-u4m4z","title":"[patterns] gemini.session.resume_hint lacks near-miss corpus freeze for resume anchor","description":"Rule gemini.session.resume_hint in crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs matches broad 'gemini resume' / 'gemini --resume' text and currently only has positive corpus fixtures (session_resume_hint.txt + session_resume_hint_v2.txt). Add a negative near-miss corpus pair to prevent false positives on docs/help text or malformed session IDs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:27:26.597674Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:32:09.604955Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:32:09.604621Z","close_reason":"Near-miss corpus added in f684c840: session_resume_hint_near_miss.{txt,expect.json} with six realistic false-positive candidates (help-text <session-id> placeholder, short-id 'abc', quoted docs backtick form, gemini-cli variant, single-quoted reference, flag form followed by non-hex word). Each line has a specific reason why either the anchor or the regex at patterns.rs:2067-2077 fails; expect.json is []. Verified locally with a Python regex equivalent: global search returns None, per-line anchor/regex trace shows every anchor-matching line is regex=False. Matches the existing near-miss pattern established by ft-8ku8p (model_used) and the gemini oauth/rate-limit/session_summary negatives.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-uic0x","title":"termwiz windows terminal: native renderer alternate screen is a no-op","description":"Production path: frankenterm/termwiz/src/terminal/windows.rs enter_alternate_screen/exit_alternate_screen return Ok for non-Terminfo renderers while TODOs say CreateConsoleScreenBuffer/SetConsoleActiveScreenBuffer are unimplemented. Impact: Windows native console sessions report success but do not enter or exit an alternate screen, so full-screen TUIs can corrupt main-buffer scrollback/state. Acceptance: implement the native alternate screen buffer path or return an explicit unsupported error instead of a silent Ok, with tests or platform-gated coverage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:40:49.306061Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:40:49.306061Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-uic0x","title":"termwiz windows terminal: native renderer alternate screen is a no-op","description":"Production path: frankenterm/termwiz/src/terminal/windows.rs enter_alternate_screen/exit_alternate_screen return Ok for non-Terminfo renderers while TODOs say CreateConsoleScreenBuffer/SetConsoleActiveScreenBuffer are unimplemented. Impact: Windows native console sessions report success but do not enter or exit an alternate screen, so full-screen TUIs can corrupt main-buffer scrollback/state. Acceptance: implement the native alternate screen buffer path or return an explicit unsupported error instead of a silent Ok, with tests or platform-gated coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:40:49.306061Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:18:35.200237Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:18:35.199956Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 33dab3e2: enter_alternate_screen and exit_alternate_screen now bail! with a named-bead message for the WindowsConsoleRenderer branch instead of silently returning Ok. Drop path stays quiet when in_alternate_screen=false. cargo check -p termwiz clean on aarch64-apple-darwin (unix path); cross-target Windows verification deferred to CI.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-uigpj","title":"diagnostic.rs: DiagnosticOptions struct literals missing include_full_cwd (2 sites)","description":"diagnostic.rs:1817 and :2711 both construct DiagnosticOptions via struct literal but omit the include_full_cwd field that was added to the struct definition. E0063 blocks cargo check/build of frankenterm-core lib. Fix: add include_full_cwd: false (or appropriate default) to both constructors, or switch to ..Default::default() base.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:43.800293Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:10:02.079758Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:10:02.079437Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-uj5c6","title":"patterns.rs dedup compile break blocks qiba0 MCP golden harness","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T07:04:07.653677Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:22:43.810694Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:22:43.810041Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5dcea11e","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-umue3","title":"recording frame writers truncate payload_len above u32::MAX","description":"Fresh-eyes review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs and replay.rs: production frame constructors use payload.len() as u32 for WAR frame headers. A captured, event, or redacted export payload larger than the on-disk 32-bit frame limit silently wraps the header length, producing a corrupt recording/replay stream instead of a clear error. Fix by centralizing checked conversion for frame payload lengths and using it at production frame-construction sites, with a unit regression for the overflow guard.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:04:17.099266Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:35:41.487702Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:35:41.487325Z","close_reason":"Fixed WAR frame payload length truncation by adding checked u32 conversion at recording and redacted replay frame construction sites. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check recording.rs replay.rs; git diff --check recording.rs replay.rs; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core checked_frame_payload_len --lib (1 passed); rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core redact --lib (260 passed); ubs --only=rust recording.rs and replay.rs exit 0.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["recording","reliability","review"]}
